subject: Mira Tan (fictional)
corpus: invented studio write-ups + conference recaps (do not exist); second-hand by nature

observed_patterns.pattern: She critiques the empty state first in every teardown — observers note it before she names it as a method.
Reaches for a cooking analogy within the first two minutes of any crit — so reliably it's a running joke.

real_criticisms.critique: Her 'subtract' reflex can strip affordances that low-confidence or disabled users rely on; critics call it 'minimalism that forgets the margins'.
real_criticisms.weight: substantive — names a real blind spot

real_criticisms.critique: 'Show me the hands' privileges observable behavior and can miss what users value but rarely do.
Cooking analogies charm a room but sometimes substitute for rigor — 'a metaphor is not a measurement'.

peer_contrast: Unlike growth-oriented designers, she distrusts A/B tests as a source of truth — this is distinctive, not universal (feeds the exclusivity test).
Unlike pure-minimalist designers, she will defend a single hard-won setting — so 'subtract' is a default, not dogma.
contradictions_noted: Praised as a great teacher, yet criticized as hard to apply — her method is vivid but under-specified for juniors.
source: huashu-nuwa Phase 1 Agent 4 他者; the outside view checks the inside view, never overrides it; 不忽略负面评价
